name: Deploy Cloud Functions from the CLI
description: Create and deploy serverless functions on Google Cloud Functions with HTTP and event-based triggers.

steps.title: Enable the Cloud Functions API
steps.description: Enables Cloud Functions and Cloud Build APIs.

steps.title: Create a function source file
steps.command: mkdir my-function && echo 'exports.helloWorld = (req, res) => { res.send("Hello from Cloud Functions!"); };' > my-function/index.js

steps.title: Deploy the function
steps.command: gcloud functions deploy helloWorld --runtime nodejs20 --trigger-http --allow-unauthenticated --source ./my-function --region us-central1 --gen2
steps.description: Deploys a 2nd-gen Cloud Function with an HTTP trigger accessible without authentication.
steps.tip: Always use --gen2 for new functions. Gen 2 runs on Cloud Run infrastructure with better performance.

steps.title: Test the function
steps.description: Invokes the function and prints the response.

steps.title: View function logs
steps.command: gcloud functions logs read helloWorld --region us-central1 --gen2 --limit 20
steps.description: Displays the most recent 20 log entries for the function.

fullScript:
# Deploy Cloud Functions
gcloud services enable cloudfunctions.googleapis.com cloudbuild.googleapis.com
mkdir -p my-function
echo 'exports.helloWorld = (req, res) => { res.send("Hello from Cloud Functions!"); };' > my-function/index.js
gcloud functions deploy helloWorld --runtime nodejs20 --trigger-http --allow-unauthenticated --source ./my-function --region us-central1 --gen2
gcloud functions call helloWorld --region us-central1 --gen2
gcloud functions logs read helloWorld --region us-central1 --gen2 --limit 20

faq.question: What event triggers are supported?
faq.answer: Cloud Functions supports HTTP triggers, Pub/Sub messages, Cloud Storage events, Firestore changes, Firebase Auth events, and Eventarc triggers for 90+ Google Cloud event sources.

faq.question: What is the maximum execution time for a Cloud Function?
faq.answer: Gen 2 functions can run up to 60 minutes. Gen 1 functions are limited to 9 minutes. Configure with --timeout flag.

faq.question: How do I set environment variables?
faq.answer: Use '--set-env-vars KEY1=val1,KEY2=val2' during deploy, or '--update-env-vars' to change them without redeploying code.
